title: PDR COURT Arbitration Rules for effective settlements
description: PDR COURT Arbitration Rules are set in order to independently, impartially, efficiently & economically resolve disputes arising from business transactions
excerpt: PDR COURT Arbitration Rules are set in order to independently, impartially, efficiently & economically resolve disputes arising from business transactions

blocks.text: Arbitration Rules
Got any Questions

blocks.html: Write to us
blocks.text: Write to us
Chapter 1: General Provisions
Article 1 – Purpose and Scope of Application

blocks.html: These Rules have been formulated to ensure the independent, impartial, efficient, and cost- effective resolution of disputes arising out of contractual or non-contractual economic and commercial transactions through arbitration.
blocks.text: These Rules have been formulated to ensure the independent, impartial, efficient, and cost- effective resolution of disputes arising out of contractual or non-contractual economic and commercial transactions through arbitration.

blocks.html: These Rules shall apply to the resolution of contractual and commercial disputes. Subject to mutual agreement of the parties, they may also be applied to the resolution of other types of economic or trade-related disputes.
blocks.text: These Rules shall apply to the resolution of contractual and commercial disputes. Subject to mutual agreement of the parties, they may also be applied to the resolution of other types of economic or trade-related disputes.
Article 2 – Definitions

items.html: “PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT” refers to the Arbitration Facilitation Platform established to administer arbitrations. Upon appointment of the Arbitrator, both the Platform and the Sole Arbitrator shall collectively be referred to as the “Arbitral Tribunal.”
items.text: “PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT” refers to the Arbitration Facilitation Platform established to administer arbitrations. Upon appointment of the Arbitrator, both the Platform and the Sole Arbitrator shall collectively be referred to as the “Arbitral Tribunal.”

items.html: “PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT Arbitration Rules” refers to the current version of these Rules, which are to be read in conjunction with the UNCITRAL Model Law on International Commercial Arbitration and the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended from time to time.
items.text: “PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT Arbitration Rules” refers to the current version of these Rules, which are to be read in conjunction with the UNCITRAL Model Law on International Commercial Arbitration and the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended from time to time.

items.html: “PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT Panel of Arbitrators” refers to the list of arbitrators maintained and published by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT, accessible for verification.
items.text: “PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT Panel of Arbitrators” refers to the list of arbitrators maintained and published by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT, accessible for verification.

items.html: “Dispute Resolution Facilitation Platform” refers to the specialized arbitration administration service operated by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.
items.text: “Dispute Resolution Facilitation Platform” refers to the specialized arbitration administration service operated by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.

items.html: “Dispute Resolution Platform Website” refers to the designated web portals managed by the Platform for the purpose of conducting online dispute resolution. The current websites are: https://www.pdrcourt.com
items.text: “Dispute Resolution Platform Website” refers to the designated web portals managed by the Platform for the purpose of conducting online dispute resolution. The current websites are: https://www.pdrcourt.com

items.html: “Written Form” includes any medium that records or transmits information, such as contracts, letters, telegrams, telexes, faxes, electronic data interchange (EDI), and emails, which are accessible for future reference.
items.text: “Written Form” includes any medium that records or transmits information, such as contracts, letters, telegrams, telexes, faxes, electronic data interchange (EDI), and emails, which are accessible for future reference.

items.html: “Electronic Evidence” means data messages generated, sent, received, or stored by electronic, optical, magnetic, or similar means, admissible under applicable laws.
items.text: “Electronic Evidence” means data messages generated, sent, received, or stored by electronic, optical, magnetic, or similar means, admissible under applicable laws.

items.html: “Electronic Signature” refers to data in electronic form that is attached to or logically associated with a data message and is used to identify the signatory and indicate their approval of the content.
items.text: “Electronic Signature” refers to data in electronic form that is attached to or logically associated with a data message and is used to identify the signatory and indicate their approval of the content.

items.html: “Online Oral Hearing” means an oral hearing conducted through online platforms, including video conferencing or other digital communication technologies.
items.text: “Online Oral Hearing” means an oral hearing conducted through online platforms, including video conferencing or other digital communication technologies.

blocks.text: Article 3 – Application of the Rules

blocks.html: These Rules shall apply to all arbitration proceedings administered by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT. In the absence of such agreement, the parties may agree to the application of these Rules at any stage, or alternatively, any other procedural rules as mutually agreed.
blocks.text: These Rules shall apply to all arbitration proceedings administered by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT. In the absence of such agreement, the parties may agree to the application of these Rules at any stage, or alternatively, any other procedural rules as mutually agreed.
Article 4 – Party Autonomy

blocks.html: The parties may agree to modify the provisions of these Rules. Such agreement shall prevail unless it is inoperative or contrary to any mandatory provision of the law applicable to the arbitration.
blocks.text: The parties may agree to modify the provisions of these Rules. Such agreement shall prevail unless it is inoperative or contrary to any mandatory provision of the law applicable to the arbitration.
Article 5 – Reference to PDR COURT

blocks.html: Where the parties have agreed to submit their disputes to arbitration under these Rules but have not designated a specific arbitration institution, they shall be deemed to have agreed to refer the dispute to arbitration administered by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.
blocks.text: Where the parties have agreed to submit their disputes to arbitration under these Rules but have not designated a specific arbitration institution, they shall be deemed to have agreed to refer the dispute to arbitration administered by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.
Article 6 – Arbitration Agreement

blocks.html: An “Arbitration Agreement” refers to any written clause in a contract or a separate agreement by which the parties agree to submit existing or future disputes to arbitration. The agreement must be in writing and may be contained in any tangible or electronic medium, including contracts, correspondence, EDI, or emails.
blocks.text: An “Arbitration Agreement” refers to any written clause in a contract or a separate agreement by which the parties agree to submit existing or future disputes to arbitration. The agreement must be in writing and may be contained in any tangible or electronic medium, including contracts, correspondence, EDI, or emails.
Article 7 – Jurisdiction and Validity of Arbitration Agreement

blocks.html: PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall have the authority to determine the existence, validity, and scope of the arbitration agreement and its own jurisdiction over the dispute. This authority may also be delegated to the Arbitral Tribunal.
blocks.text: PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall have the authority to determine the existence, validity, and scope of the arbitration agreement and its own jurisdiction over the dispute. This authority may also be delegated to the Arbitral Tribunal.

blocks.html: Arbitration proceedings shall not be interrupted by any objections raised by a party regarding the validity of the arbitration agreement or the jurisdiction of the Arbitral Tribunal.
blocks.text: Arbitration proceedings shall not be interrupted by any objections raised by a party regarding the validity of the arbitration agreement or the jurisdiction of the Arbitral Tribunal.
Article 8 – Place of Arbitration

blocks.html: Where the parties have agreed on the seat or place of arbitration, their agreement shall prevail. In the absence of such agreement, the place of arbitration shall be determined by the Registrar or the appointed Arbitrator of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.
blocks.text: Where the parties have agreed on the seat or place of arbitration, their agreement shall prevail. In the absence of such agreement, the place of arbitration shall be determined by the Registrar or the appointed Arbitrator of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.

blocks.html: The arbitral award shall be deemed to have been made at the designated place of arbitration.
blocks.text: The arbitral award shall be deemed to have been made at the designated place of arbitration.
Article 9 – Communication in Writing

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or determined by the Arbitral Tribunal, any communication, notice, or submission made by post or email shall be deemed to be in writing, provided that evidence of transmission can be demonstrated.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or determined by the Arbitral Tribunal, any communication, notice, or submission made by post or email shall be deemed to be in writing, provided that evidence of transmission can be demonstrated.
Chapter 2: Communications
Article 10 – Modes of Communication

blocks.html: All documents, notices, and written communications relating to the arbitration shall be transmitted by the Arbitration Tribunal to the parties or their duly authorized representatives via email, electronic data interchange (EDI), or other comparable electronic means.
blocks.text: All documents, notices, and written communications relating to the arbitration shall be transmitted by the Arbitration Tribunal to the parties or their duly authorized representatives via email, electronic data interchange (EDI), or other comparable electronic means.

blocks.html: Subject to the specific requirements of the proceedings, the Arbitration Tribunal or the Arbitral Tribunal may additionally determine that such documents be served, either primarily or supplementally, through registered post, courier, or any other appropriate means.
blocks.text: Subject to the specific requirements of the proceedings, the Arbitration Tribunal or the Arbitral Tribunal may additionally determine that such documents be served, either primarily or supplementally, through registered post, courier, or any other appropriate means.

blocks.html: Parties shall submit all arbitration-related documents—including the Request for Arbitration, Statement of Claim, Statement of Defence, written submissions, documentary evidence, and other materials—either via email, postal service, or through the online dispute resolution platform maintained by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.
blocks.text: Parties shall submit all arbitration-related documents—including the Request for Arbitration, Statement of Claim, Statement of Defence, written submissions, documentary evidence, and other materials—either via email, postal service, or through the online dispute resolution platform maintained by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.

blocks.html: Submission via any other medium may be permitted with the prior approval of the Arbitration Tribunal or the Arbitral Tribunal.
blocks.text: Submission via any other medium may be permitted with the prior approval of the Arbitration Tribunal or the Arbitral Tribunal.
Article 11 – Communication Requirements and Conduct

blocks.html: Any document transmitted by the Arbitration Tribunal to one party shall, where applicable, be simultaneously copied to the other party. No party or its representative shall engage in unilateral communication with any member of the Arbitral Tribunal. All correspondence shall be directed through the Arbitration Tribunal. It shall be the responsibility of the sender to maintain records evidencing the dispatch and transmission of all documents. Such records shall be available for inspection and reporting, if required. In the event of non-delivery or failure of transmission, the sending party shall promptly notify the Arbitration Tribunal, which shall direct further steps as deemed appropriate. Any change in a party’s communication address, email, phone number, or other contact details must be notified immediately to the Arbitration Tribunal.
blocks.text: Any document transmitted by the Arbitration Tribunal to one party shall, where applicable, be simultaneously copied to the other party. No party or its representative shall engage in unilateral communication with any member of the Arbitral Tribunal. All correspondence shall be directed through the Arbitration Tribunal. It shall be the responsibility of the sender to maintain records evidencing the dispatch and transmission of all documents. Such records shall be available for inspection and reporting, if required. In the event of non-delivery or failure of transmission, the sending party shall promptly notify the Arbitration Tribunal, which shall direct further steps as deemed appropriate. Any change in a party’s communication address, email, phone number, or other contact details must be notified immediately to the Arbitration Tribunal.
Article 12 – Methods of Service

blocks.html: Unless a party specifies its preferred method of service in advance, any written communication to the Claimant or Respondent shall be transmitted through one or more of the following methods, as deemed appropriate by the Arbitration Tribunal:
blocks.text: Unless a party specifies its preferred method of service in advance, any written communication to the Claimant or Respondent shall be transmitted through one or more of the following methods, as deemed appropriate by the Arbitration Tribunal:

items.html: By registered post or courier with tracking capabilities;
items.text: By registered post or courier with tracking capabilities;

items.html: By email to the address on record;
items.text: By email to the address on record;

items.html: By messaging applications (such as WhatsApp) in exceptional circumstances and upon confirmation of delivery;
items.text: By messaging applications (such as WhatsApp) in exceptional circumstances and upon confirmation of delivery;

items.html: By any other effective means capable of ensuring delivery.
items.text: By any other effective means capable of ensuring delivery.

blocks.text: Article 13 – Deemed Receipt

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or directed by the Arbitral Tribunal, any communication transmitted in accordance with Article 12 shall be deemed to have been received on the date it is sent, provided there is proof of such dispatch and the medium used permits confirmation of delivery.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or directed by the Arbitral Tribunal, any communication transmitted in accordance with Article 12 shall be deemed to have been received on the date it is sent, provided there is proof of such dispatch and the medium used permits confirmation of delivery.
Article 14 – Calculation of Time Periods

blocks.html: Except as otherwise provided in these Rules, any time period stipulated herein shall commence from the earliest date on which the relevant communication is deemed to have been received in accordance with Article 13.
blocks.text: Except as otherwise provided in these Rules, any time period stipulated herein shall commence from the earliest date on which the relevant communication is deemed to have been received in accordance with Article 13.
Article 15 – Data Security and Online Transmission

blocks.html: PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall undertake reasonable security measures to protect the online transmission and storage of case-related data. This includes the use of encryption and other security protocols to safeguard communications between the parties, the Arbitral Tribunal, and the institution.
blocks.text: PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall undertake reasonable security measures to protect the online transmission and storage of case-related data. This includes the use of encryption and other security protocols to safeguard communications between the parties, the Arbitral Tribunal, and the institution.
Article 16 – Limitation of Liability for Data Transmission

blocks.html: PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall not be liable for any loss, damage, or disclosure resulting from unauthorized access to electronically transmitted data caused by technical failures, system vulnerabilities, or security breaches during arbitration proceedings.
blocks.text: PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall not be liable for any loss, damage, or disclosure resulting from unauthorized access to electronically transmitted data caused by technical failures, system vulnerabilities, or security breaches during arbitration proceedings.

blocks.html: The parties expressly waive and release PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT, its officers, employees, arbitrators, agents, and affiliates from any liability, claim, or damages arising in connection with data loss, breach, or failure of the electronic systems used in the arbitration.
blocks.text: The parties expressly waive and release PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT, its officers, employees, arbitrators, agents, and affiliates from any liability, claim, or damages arising in connection with data loss, breach, or failure of the electronic systems used in the arbitration.
Article 16A – Confidentiality

blocks.html: The parties agree that the arbitration proceedings shall remain confidential. No party shall disclose any aspect of the arbitration—including pleadings, evidence, orders, or the final award—to any third party, except their legal counsel or as required by law, regulatory obligation, or for the enforcement of an arbitral award.
blocks.text: The parties agree that the arbitration proceedings shall remain confidential. No party shall disclose any aspect of the arbitration—including pleadings, evidence, orders, or the final award—to any third party, except their legal counsel or as required by law, regulatory obligation, or for the enforcement of an arbitral award.
Article 16B – Waiver of Objection to Rules

blocks.html: The parties expressly waive any right to challenge the validity or enforceability of these Rules on the grounds of lack of knowledge or familiarity. By agreeing to arbitrate under these Rules, the parties acknowledge that they are bound by the procedural framework herein contained.
blocks.text: The parties expressly waive any right to challenge the validity or enforceability of these Rules on the grounds of lack of knowledge or familiarity. By agreeing to arbitrate under these Rules, the parties acknowledge that they are bound by the procedural framework herein contained.
Chapter 3: Arbitral Proceedings
Section 1: Request for Arbitration, Claim, Defence and Counterclaim
Article 17 – Commencement of Arbitration

blocks.html: Arbitral proceedings shall be deemed to commence on the date the Arbitration Tribunal receives the Request for Arbitration from the Claimant.
blocks.text: Arbitral proceedings shall be deemed to commence on the date the Arbitration Tribunal receives the Request for Arbitration from the Claimant.
Article 18 – Requirements for Request for Arbitration

items.html: Full names, addresses, PIN codes, telephone numbers, email addresses, and other available modes of communication of both the Claimant and the Respondent;
items.text: Full names, addresses, PIN codes, telephone numbers, email addresses, and other available modes of communication of both the Claimant and the Respondent;

items.html: The Claimant’s designated method of communication;
items.text: The Claimant’s designated method of communication;

items.html: Reference to the arbitration agreement invoked;
items.text: Reference to the arbitration agreement invoked;

items.html: A statement of claim, including the relief sought;
items.text: A statement of claim, including the relief sought;

items.html: A concise statement of facts and the principal issues in dispute; and The factual and legal grounds on which the claim is based.
items.text: A concise statement of facts and the principal issues in dispute; and The factual and legal grounds on which the claim is based.

items.html: The Claimant shall also: Attach relevant documentary evidence supporting the claim; Pay the prescribed arbitration fee in advance as per the Payment Schedule provided by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.
items.text: The Claimant shall also: Attach relevant documentary evidence supporting the claim; Pay the prescribed arbitration fee in advance as per the Payment Schedule provided by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.

blocks.text: Article 19 – Scrutiny of Request

blocks.html: Upon receipt of the Request for Arbitration, the Arbitration Tribunal shall examine whether the Request complies with the procedural requirements. Within fifteen (15) days of receipt, the Tribunal shall either:
blocks.text: Upon receipt of the Request for Arbitration, the Arbitration Tribunal shall examine whether the Request complies with the procedural requirements. Within fifteen (15) days of receipt, the Tribunal shall either:

blocks.html: Confirm its acceptance of the Request and notify the parties in writing, or
blocks.text: Confirm its acceptance of the Request and notify the parties in writing, or

blocks.html: Reject the Request, stating the reasons for non-acceptance in writing.
blocks.text: Reject the Request, stating the reasons for non-acceptance in writing.
Article 20 – Notice of Arbitration

blocks.html: Following acceptance of the Request, the Arbitration Tribunal shall issue a formal Notice of Arbitration to both parties. The Notice shall:
blocks.text: Following acceptance of the Request, the Arbitration Tribunal shall issue a formal Notice of Arbitration to both parties. The Notice shall:

blocks.html: Confirm acceptance of the arbitration;
blocks.text: Confirm acceptance of the arbitration;

blocks.html: Provide the web address(es), email, and contact details of the Dispute Resolution Facilitation Platform;
blocks.text: Provide the web address(es), email, and contact details of the Dispute Resolution Facilitation Platform;

blocks.html: Include directions for accessing these Rules and the Panel of Arbitrators;
blocks.text: Include directions for accessing these Rules and the Panel of Arbitrators;

blocks.html: Where appropriate, attach the Rules, Panel of Arbitrators, and relevant procedural documents.
blocks.text: Where appropriate, attach the Rules, Panel of Arbitrators, and relevant procedural documents.
Article 21 – Statement of Claim

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the Claimant shall submit its detailed Statement of Claim along with supporting evidence to the Arbitration Tribunal within fifteen (15) days of receipt of the Acceptance Letter.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the Claimant shall submit its detailed Statement of Claim along with supporting evidence to the Arbitration Tribunal within fifteen (15) days of receipt of the Acceptance Letter.
Article 21A – Statement of Defence and Counterclaim

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the Respondent shall submit its Statement of Defence within fifteen (15) days of receiving the Statement of Claim. The Statement of Defence, duly signed or sealed by the Respondent or its authorised representative(s), shall include:
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the Respondent shall submit its Statement of Defence within fifteen (15) days of receiving the Statement of Claim. The Statement of Defence, duly signed or sealed by the Respondent or its authorised representative(s), shall include:

blocks.html: Full names, addresses, PIN codes, telephone numbers, email addresses, and other available modes of communication of the Respondent;
blocks.text: Full names, addresses, PIN codes, telephone numbers, email addresses, and other available modes of communication of the Respondent;

blocks.html: The Respondent’s designated method of communication;
blocks.text: The Respondent’s designated method of communication;

blocks.html: A statement of defence setting forth the facts and grounds relied upon; and Supporting evidence for the defence.
blocks.text: A statement of defence setting forth the facts and grounds relied upon; and Supporting evidence for the defence.

blocks.html: If the Respondent intends to file a Counterclaim, it shall do so within the same time period unless otherwise agreed.
blocks.text: If the Respondent intends to file a Counterclaim, it shall do so within the same time period unless otherwise agreed.
Article 22 – Defence to Counterclaim

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the Claimant shall file its Statement of Defence to the Counterclaim with the Arbitration Tribunal within fifteen (15) days of receipt of the Respondent’s Counterclaim.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the Claimant shall file its Statement of Defence to the Counterclaim with the Arbitration Tribunal within fifteen (15) days of receipt of the Respondent’s Counterclaim.
Article 23 – Extension of Time

blocks.html: The time limits prescribed under Articles 21 and 22 may be extended by the Arbitral Tribunal upon a showing of sufficient cause or for reasons the Tribunal considers just and equitable.
blocks.text: The time limits prescribed under Articles 21 and 22 may be extended by the Arbitral Tribunal upon a showing of sufficient cause or for reasons the Tribunal considers just and equitable.
Article 23A – Language of Arbitration

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties in writing, the language of the arbitration shall be English.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties in writing, the language of the arbitration shall be English.
Article 23B – Interim Relief

blocks.html: Either party may apply to the Arbitral Tribunal for interim measures of protection by filing an application under Section 17 of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The Arbitral Tribunal shall have the same powers to grant interim relief as a civil court under applicable law. Subject to any orders passed under Section 37 of the Act, any interim order made by the Arbitral Tribunal shall be deemed to be an order of the court and shall be enforceable under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as though it were a court order.
blocks.text: Either party may apply to the Arbitral Tribunal for interim measures of protection by filing an application under Section 17 of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The Arbitral Tribunal shall have the same powers to grant interim relief as a civil court under applicable law. Subject to any orders passed under Section 37 of the Act, any interim order made by the Arbitral Tribunal shall be deemed to be an order of the court and shall be enforceable under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as though it were a court order.
Article 23C – Ex-parte Proceedings

blocks.html: Within thirty (30) days of receipt of the arbitral award, unless otherwise agreed by the parties:
blocks.text: Within thirty (30) days of receipt of the arbitral award, unless otherwise agreed by the parties:

blocks.html: a. A party may request the Arbitral Tribunal to correct any clerical, typographical, or computational errors in the award;
blocks.text: a. A party may request the Arbitral Tribunal to correct any clerical, typographical, or computational errors in the award;

blocks.html: b. If mutually agreed, a party may also request an interpretation of a specific portion of the award.
blocks.text: b. If mutually agreed, a party may also request an interpretation of a specific portion of the award.

blocks.html: c. If the Arbitral Tribunal finds the request justified, it shall make the correction or provide the interpretation within thirty (30) days of receipt. The interpretation shall form part of the original award.
blocks.text: c. If the Arbitral Tribunal finds the request justified, it shall make the correction or provide the interpretation within thirty (30) days of receipt. The interpretation shall form part of the original award.

blocks.html: d. The Tribunal may, on its own initiative, correct such errors within thirty (30) days of the award.
blocks.text: d. The Tribunal may, on its own initiative, correct such errors within thirty (30) days of the award.

blocks.html: e. A party may also request an additional award within thirty (30) days of receipt of the original award concerning claims raised during the proceedings but omitted from the award.
blocks.text: e. A party may also request an additional award within thirty (30) days of receipt of the original award concerning claims raised during the proceedings but omitted from the award.

blocks.html: f. If the Tribunal deems such request valid, it shall issue the additional award within sixty (60) days of the request.
blocks.text: f. If the Tribunal deems such request valid, it shall issue the additional award within sixty (60) days of the request.

blocks.html: g. The Tribunal may extend the aforementioned periods where necessary.
blocks.text: g. The Tribunal may extend the aforementioned periods where necessary.

blocks.html: h. The Tribunal may levy additional fees for such corrections, interpretations, or additional awards, as it deems appropriate.
blocks.text: h. The Tribunal may levy additional fees for such corrections, interpretations, or additional awards, as it deems appropriate.
Section 2: The Arbitral Tribunal
Article 24 – Composition of the Arbitral Tribunal

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or stipulated under these Rules, the arbitral tribunal shall consist of a sole arbitrator.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or stipulated under these Rules, the arbitral tribunal shall consist of a sole arbitrator.
Article 25 – Appointment of the Arbitrator

blocks.html: The arbitrator shall be appointed in accordance with the provisions of the arbitration agreement or arbitration clause executed between the parties. Unless the parties agree otherwise in writing, the arbitrator shall be selected from the Panel of Arbitrators maintained by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.
blocks.text: The arbitrator shall be appointed in accordance with the provisions of the arbitration agreement or arbitration clause executed between the parties. Unless the parties agree otherwise in writing, the arbitrator shall be selected from the Panel of Arbitrators maintained by PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.

blocks.html: Where the parties fail to appoint an arbitrator or agree on one, and where the arbitration agreement authorizes institutional appointment, the Registrar of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall appoint the arbitrator from its Panel of Arbitrators, unless otherwise agreed by the parties.
blocks.text: Where the parties fail to appoint an arbitrator or agree on one, and where the arbitration agreement authorizes institutional appointment, the Registrar of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall appoint the arbitrator from its Panel of Arbitrators, unless otherwise agreed by the parties.
Article 26 – Declaration of Independence and Impartiality

blocks.html: Every arbitrator, whether appointed by the parties or by the Registrar, shall, prior to appointment, execute a written Declaration of Independence and Impartiality. Such declaration shall disclose any past or present circumstances likely to give rise to justifiable doubts as to the arbitrator’s impartiality or independence. The declaration shall be submitted to PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT, which shall communicate the same to the parties.
blocks.text: Every arbitrator, whether appointed by the parties or by the Registrar, shall, prior to appointment, execute a written Declaration of Independence and Impartiality. Such declaration shall disclose any past or present circumstances likely to give rise to justifiable doubts as to the arbitrator’s impartiality or independence. The declaration shall be submitted to PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT, which shall communicate the same to the parties.
Article 27 – Procedure for Appointment of Sole Arbitrator

blocks.html: Where the tribunal is to be composed of a sole arbitrator and the parties have not agreed on the manner of appointment, the Claimant and Respondent shall, within fifteen (15) days from the date the party last receives the Notice of Arbitration, jointly appoint a sole arbitrator.
blocks.text: Where the tribunal is to be composed of a sole arbitrator and the parties have not agreed on the manner of appointment, the Claimant and Respondent shall, within fifteen (15) days from the date the party last receives the Notice of Arbitration, jointly appoint a sole arbitrator.

blocks.html: In the event that the parties fail to jointly appoint a sole arbitrator within the stipulated time or do not request the Registrar to make the appointment, the Registrar of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall appoint the sole arbitrator from the institution’s Panel of Arbitrators.
blocks.text: In the event that the parties fail to jointly appoint a sole arbitrator within the stipulated time or do not request the Registrar to make the appointment, the Registrar of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall appoint the sole arbitrator from the institution’s Panel of Arbitrators.

blocks.html: The time period prescribed in this Article may be extended upon mutual consent of the Registrar and the arbitrator, considering the circumstances of the case.
blocks.text: The time period prescribed in this Article may be extended upon mutual consent of the Registrar and the arbitrator, considering the circumstances of the case.
Section 3: Hearing
Article 28 – Conduct of Proceedings

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, and subject to these Rules, the Arbitral Tribunal shall have the discretion to conduct the arbitration in such manner as it deems appropriate, ensuring procedural fairness and efficiency. The Arbitral Tribunal shall, at all times, treat the parties equally and provide each party a reasonable opportunity to present its case.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, and subject to these Rules, the Arbitral Tribunal shall have the discretion to conduct the arbitration in such manner as it deems appropriate, ensuring procedural fairness and efficiency. The Arbitral Tribunal shall, at all times, treat the parties equally and provide each party a reasonable opportunity to present its case.

blocks.html: The Arbitral Tribunal may issue procedural directions, including procedural timetables, lists of issues, and terms of reference, to facilitate efficient conduct of the proceedings.
blocks.text: The Arbitral Tribunal may issue procedural directions, including procedural timetables, lists of issues, and terms of reference, to facilitate efficient conduct of the proceedings.

blocks.html: The Arbitral Tribunal shall have the authority to determine the admissibility, relevance, materiality, and weight of all evidence submitted.
blocks.text: The Arbitral Tribunal shall have the authority to determine the admissibility, relevance, materiality, and weight of all evidence submitted.
Article 29 – Electronic Evidence

blocks.html: The evidence submitted by the parties may be electronic evidence that is generated, sent, received or stored by electronic, optical, magnetic or other similar means.
blocks.text: The evidence submitted by the parties may be electronic evidence that is generated, sent, received or stored by electronic, optical, magnetic or other similar means.

items.html: The reliability of the method used to generate, store, or transmit the electronic data;
items.text: The reliability of the method used to generate, store, or transmit the electronic data;

items.html: The reliability of the method used to ensure the integrity of the content;
items.text: The reliability of the method used to ensure the integrity of the content;

items.html: The reliability of the method used to identify the originator; and Any other relevant factors.
items.text: The reliability of the method used to identify the originator; and Any other relevant factors.

items.html: Electronic evidence bearing a reliable electronic signature shall have the same legal effect as evidence with a handwritten signature or an affixed seal.
items.text: Electronic evidence bearing a reliable electronic signature shall have the same legal effect as evidence with a handwritten signature or an affixed seal.

blocks.text: Article 30 – Submission of Evidence

blocks.html: Where a time limit has been set by the parties or the Arbitral Tribunal for the submission of evidence, such evidence shall be filed within the stipulated period.
blocks.text: Where a time limit has been set by the parties or the Arbitral Tribunal for the submission of evidence, such evidence shall be filed within the stipulated period.

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or directed by the Arbitral Tribunal, the Tribunal may, in its discretion, decline to admit any evidence or written submission submitted after the expiration of such period. However, the Tribunal may request additional documents or statements if deemed necessary for the resolution of the dispute.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or directed by the Arbitral Tribunal, the Tribunal may, in its discretion, decline to admit any evidence or written submission submitted after the expiration of such period. However, the Tribunal may request additional documents or statements if deemed necessary for the resolution of the dispute.
Article 31 – Minimum Number of Hearings

blocks.html: The Arbitration Tribunal shall conduct a minimum of three (3) hearings prior to issuing an arbitral award, unless otherwise agreed by the parties or deemed unnecessary due to default or ex-parte proceedings.
blocks.text: The Arbitration Tribunal shall conduct a minimum of three (3) hearings prior to issuing an arbitral award, unless otherwise agreed by the parties or deemed unnecessary due to default or ex-parte proceedings.
Article 32 – Mode of Oral Hearings

blocks.html: Where an oral hearing is required, it shall, by default, be conducted online through video conferencing or similar electronic communication tools. The Arbitral Tribunal may, based on the specific facts and circumstances, decide to conduct an in-person (physical) hearing.
blocks.text: Where an oral hearing is required, it shall, by default, be conducted online through video conferencing or similar electronic communication tools. The Arbitral Tribunal may, based on the specific facts and circumstances, decide to conduct an in-person (physical) hearing.
Article 33 – Notice and Postponement of Hearing

blocks.html: The Arbitral Tribunal shall fix the date, time, mode, and location (if applicable) of any oral hearing and shall serve a Notice of Oral Hearing to all parties at least three (3) days in advance.
blocks.text: The Arbitral Tribunal shall fix the date, time, mode, and location (if applicable) of any oral hearing and shall serve a Notice of Oral Hearing to all parties at least three (3) days in advance.

blocks.html: Any request for postponement must be submitted in writing with justifiable reasons at least five (5) days before the scheduled hearing. The Arbitral Tribunal shall have the discretion to accept or reject such requests.
blocks.text: Any request for postponement must be submitted in writing with justifiable reasons at least five (5) days before the scheduled hearing. The Arbitral Tribunal shall have the discretion to accept or reject such requests.
Article 34A – Security Deposit

blocks.html: The Arbitral Tribunal may direct the parties to make a reasonable deposit toward administrative costs, arbitrator’s fees, or any other anticipated expenses. Failure to comply with the deposit direction may result in forfeiture of the deposit and, where applicable, termination of the arbitration proceedings, upon ten (10) days' written notice.
blocks.text: The Arbitral Tribunal may direct the parties to make a reasonable deposit toward administrative costs, arbitrator’s fees, or any other anticipated expenses. Failure to comply with the deposit direction may result in forfeiture of the deposit and, where applicable, termination of the arbitration proceedings, upon ten (10) days' written notice.
Article 34D – Logistical and Administrative Assistance

blocks.html: Upon request by the arbitrator or the parties, the Registrar of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall facilitate logistical arrangements for the arbitration, including but not limited to securing venues for physical hearings. The requesting party shall bear any expenses incurred in this regard.
blocks.text: Upon request by the arbitrator or the parties, the Registrar of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT shall facilitate logistical arrangements for the arbitration, including but not limited to securing venues for physical hearings. The requesting party shall bear any expenses incurred in this regard.
Article 35 – Examination of Witnesses

blocks.html: Witness testimony may be presented by any of the following means, as deemed appropriate by the Arbitral Tribunal:
blocks.text: Witness testimony may be presented by any of the following means, as deemed appropriate by the Arbitral Tribunal:

blocks.html: Online video conferencing,
blocks.text: Online video conferencing,

blocks.html: In-person oral hearings, or
blocks.text: In-person oral hearings, or

blocks.html: Any other suitable mode of communication approved by the Tribunal.
blocks.text: Any other suitable mode of communication approved by the Tribunal.
Section 4: Arbitral Award
Article 36 – Timeframe for Award

blocks.html: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the Arbitral Tribunal shall render its final award within six (6) months from the date of its constitution.
blocks.text: Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the Arbitral Tribunal shall render its final award within six (6) months from the date of its constitution.

blocks.html: This period may be extended upon a written request from the Tribunal and approval by the Chairperson of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT, subject to justification of the necessity of such extension.
blocks.text: This period may be extended upon a written request from the Tribunal and approval by the Chairperson of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT, subject to justification of the necessity of such extension.
Article 37 – Form and Effect of Award

blocks.html: The arbitral award shall be in writing, signed by the arbitrator, and shall state the date and place of the award. The award shall bear the official seal of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.
blocks.text: The arbitral award shall be in writing, signed by the arbitrator, and shall state the date and place of the award. The award shall bear the official seal of PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT.

blocks.html: Upon issuance of the award, the arbitration proceedings shall stand terminated. The arbitrator’s mandate shall also conclude upon such termination.
blocks.text: Upon issuance of the award, the arbitration proceedings shall stand terminated. The arbitrator’s mandate shall also conclude upon such termination.
Article 38 – Scrutiny of Award

blocks.html: Prior to signing the final award, the Arbitral Tribunal shall submit a draft award to PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT for administrative scrutiny. The institution may raise any non-substantive issue with the draft for the Tribunal’s consideration, provided that the independence and decision-making authority of the Arbitral Tribunal remain unaffected.
blocks.text: Prior to signing the final award, the Arbitral Tribunal shall submit a draft award to PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT for administrative scrutiny. The institution may raise any non-substantive issue with the draft for the Tribunal’s consideration, provided that the independence and decision-making authority of the Arbitral Tribunal remain unaffected.
Article 39 – Enforcement of Award

blocks.html: The award-holder shall be entitled to seek enforcement of the arbitral award in accordance with applicable law. Enforcement shall be governed by the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, and the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
blocks.text: The award-holder shall be entitled to seek enforcement of the arbitral award in accordance with applicable law. Enforcement shall be governed by the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, and the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
Article 40 – Interpretation and Amendment of Rules

blocks.html: PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT reserves the sole right to interpret, amend, or supplement these Rules, and such interpretations or amendments shall be binding on all parties and arbitrators involved in the proceedings.
blocks.text: PDR ORGANIZATION PRIVATE LIMITED / PDR COURT reserves the sole right to interpret, amend, or supplement these Rules, and such interpretations or amendments shall be binding on all parties and arbitrators involved in the proceedings.

Career Opportunities

blocks.html: At PDR COURT, we cultivate an inclusive culture powered by our innovative solutions, fostering a genuine and impactful work environment. We celebrate diversity, ensuring everyone is respected and inspired to excel.

Our success is driven by dedicated teamwork that utilizes our PDR COURT as their trusted ADR platform to protect and promote member interests.

blocks.text: Join us at PDR COURT, where career opportunities align with your development, allowing you to thrive in a tech-driven environment. We view our employees as invaluable assets, offering growth alongside our collective success.
We seek challengers committed to independent thinking and talent development, driving client growth and securing a profitable future.
The Place of Nyaya

If you share our vision, join us!
Roles & Responsibilities

blocks.html: At PDR COURT, you will leverage our platform to facilitate dispute resolution while upholding our commitment to transparency and integrity. Your role will involve collaborating with diverse teams to enhance client experiences and drive organizational success.

You can buy Agreement using our app in 3 Ways
Our Executives, team of experts and our Support staff are here to resolve any of your issues.
Web Agreeement
Simply, generate a web link and both parties can e-sign using the link.
Digital Agreeement
Single
Enter your Details and Download the Dispute Resolution paper in pdf. Take print and send to second party for signing.
Multiple
Wish Multiple Agreements at once. Download the Excel sheet, enter details in table and upload. Download these agreements in pdf format.
Printed Agreement
Care for an offline Agreement. Buy it here and we will courier it you in your physical address.

How much time does it take to resolve a case?

OUR STORY SO FAR
Mar 2019
Bootstrapped with ad hoc investment of 15 Lac.
Mar 2020
Hired our first full-time arbitrator & Started working in 11 Languages.
Mar 2021
Received multiple disputes of INR 9 Cr. We set a target to resolve in 60 days, and we did it.
Jan 2022
Started physical conciliation centers in Mumbai & Delhi.
Feb 2023
Release of "Negotiation Techniques for NBFCs," offering specialized insights and case studies in ADR for Non-Banking Financial Companies.
June 2023
Knowledge Centre published on its website, offering a wealth of specialized resources and insights covering all facets of alternative dispute resolution methodologies.
PDR COURT 2.0 from March 2024

Download our logos, media statements, digital assets, white papers, and more—all designed for easy access and use in a tech-driven landscape.
PDR COURT
The Digital Seal

blocks.html: PDR COURT is an International Arbitration Court. The Digital Seal of PDR COURT represents justice or Nyaya in the Indic value system. The symbol incorporates the Wheel of Dharma and the Pillar of Truth, reflecting our commitment to a transparent, technology-driven process. The essence of our platform embodies the belief that "Justice is established through righteousness" (dharmeṇa nyāyaḥ sthāpyate = धर्मेण न्यायः स्थाप्यते).
blocks.text: PDR COURT is an International Arbitration Court. The Digital Seal of PDR COURT represents justice or Nyaya in the Indic value system. The symbol incorporates the Wheel of Dharma and the Pillar of Truth, reflecting our commitment to a transparent, technology-driven process. The essence of our platform embodies the belief that "Justice is established through righteousness" (dharmeṇa nyāyaḥ sthāpyate = धर्मेण न्यायः स्थाप्यते).
Core Values

blocks.html: Discipline with a tech-savvy and dynamic approach drives our solutions, leading us to the truth. PDR COURT harnesses digital innovation to reinvent and enhance the Indic logic of resolving disputes through our advanced platform.
blocks.text: Discipline with a tech-savvy and dynamic approach drives our solutions, leading us to the truth. PDR COURT harnesses digital innovation to reinvent and enhance the Indic logic of resolving disputes through our advanced platform.
Our Mission

blocks.html: PDR COURT is built on the expertise of our skilled team who manage the ADR process from start to finish. We embrace technological advancements to enhance our services, ensuring that your achievements are shared successes for us all.
blocks.text: PDR COURT is built on the expertise of our skilled team who manage the ADR process from start to finish. We embrace technological advancements to enhance our services, ensuring that your achievements are shared successes for us all.
Our Philosophy
नास्ति सत्यसमो धर्मो न सत्याद्विद्यते परम्। न हि तीव्रतरं किञ्चिद् नृतादिह विद्यते।।
Transliteration
Nāsti Satyasamo Dharmo Na Satyādvidyate Paraṁ. Na hi Tīvrataraṁ Kiñchid Nr̥tādih Vidyate.
English Transliteration
There is no other religion like truth. nothing but the truth. Nothing is more intense than a lie.
Hindi Transliteration
सत्य जैसा अन्य धर्म नहीं। सत्य से पर कुछ नहीं। असत्य से ज्यादा तीव्रतर कुछ नहीं।
